objective: Replace generic thought chips with realtime live tool and command chips driven by runtime events

summary.title: compaction
summary.body:
---
## Goal

Replace the decorative/static thinking-card chips with a realtime, truth-driven live activity HUD that reflects Heidi’s actual runtime work (tool calls, terminal commands, subagents, verify actions). Keep the premium motion/visual language and make the chip row event-driven (appear on start, hold while active, exit when finished/errored), mobile-first with caps and overflow, and instrumented with documentation and doctor checks.

## Instructions

- Work only on the thinking/theater live chip row and the minimal event plumbing needed to drive it from the runtime event stream. No unrelated refactors.
- Use the existing SSE → sync store pipeline (events → `message.part.updated` → `data.store.part`) as the authoritative source of runtime activity.
- Build a small LiveActivity model with fields id, kind, label, status, start_time, end_time, priority, source_event_id. Only render active/running + just-finished items.
- Lifecycle rules:
  - On start (pending/running): enter immediately and show running state.
  - While running: remain visible and animated.
  - On completion: show brief exit (soften/fade) then remove after a short window.
  - On error: show brief error style, then remove quickly after the exit window.
  - No polling; drive lifecycle from reactive store + single-shot "just finished" timeout windows.
- Label rules:
  - Tool chips: normalized readable tool names (no invented labels).
  - Command chips: show actual command string (or cleaned/truncated display) — truthful truncation only.
  - Subagent chips: prefer `subagent_type` label when available.
- Priority and visibility:
  - Priority order: active terminal command > verify/check > edit/write/apply_patch > task/subagent > read/search/list > others.
  - Caps per breakpoint: desktop 5, tablet 4, mobile 3.
  - Overflow: show `+N active` chip (do not render a large wrapped wall).
- Preserve the premium enter/exit motion system; map it to the new event-driven lifecycle.
- Add a clear implementation doc and a doctor check module to validate the new wiring and doc presence.

(Plan/spec used repeatedly in code: live model mapping, lifecycle timing/TTL, priority, cap rules, fallback behavior, doctor checks. See `.local/implementation-live-activity-chips.md`.)

## Discoveries

- There is a real SSE event pipeline and a reactive UI store already present:
  - upstream SSE stream in `packages/app/src/context/global-sdk.tsx` → events reduced in `global-sync` → available to UI via `DataProvider` as `data.store.part`.
  - `message.part.updated` reductions populate `data.store.part` (this is the most direct source for tool lifecycle).
- `ToolPart` types in the SDK provide the necessary fields: `id`, `callID`, `tool`, `state.status` (`pending|running|completed|error`), `state.input` (command strings), and `state.time.start/end`.
- `session-turn.tsx` is the correct, minimal-scoped insertion point: it already reads assistant messages and parts and computes `orbPhase()`; it was extended to derive live activities and pass them into `ThinkingTheater`.
- `ThinkingTheater` already has the premium motion system and was extended to accept `activities?: LiveActivity[]` and render them (with stable row height, caps, overflow chip).
- A single-shot timer approach (900ms TTL after end_time) preserves brief exit animation for completed/error chips without polling.
- The existing `thinking-wording` copy pack and title/subtext systems remain in place for titles/subtext; chips are now live-first and fallback to phase-based minimal chips only when no live activity exists.
- Doctor checks required updated patterns; the new doctor check was added and wired into `tools/doctor.py`.

## Accomplished

Work completed
- Implemented live activity derivation:
  - `session-turn.tsx` now:
    - derives a `LiveActivity[]` from `data.store.part` for the current assistant messages,
    - maps `ToolPart` → `{ id, kind, label, status, start_time, end_time, priority, source_event_id }`,
    - applies priority sorting and de-duplication,
    - retains just-finished items for a short exit window via a single-shot timeout (900ms).
- Integrated live activities into the thinking card:
  - `thinking-theater.tsx`:
    - defines `LiveActivity` type,
    - accepts `activities` prop and renders the live chip row when present,
    - enforces per-breakpoint cap logic (mobile:3, tablet:4, desktop:5) and shows `+N active` overflow,
    - provides fallback minimal phase chips when no live activity exists,
    - preserved and reused premium enter/exit motion patterns and added subtle status/kind styling attributes (`data-kind`, `data-status`) for visual hints.
- Visual scaffolding:
  - `thinking-theater.css`: made chip row non-wrapping, stable height, mono font for command chips, success/error animation hooks, running glow.
- Documentation and doctor:
  - Added `.local/implementation-live-activity-chips.md` (spec, lifecycle, priority, formatting rules).
  - Added `tools/doctor_checks/live_activity_chips.py` and wired it into `tools/doctor.py`.
- Verification:
  - `bun run --cwd packages/ui typecheck` passed.
  - `bun turbo typecheck` (workspace) passed.
  - `python3 tools/doctor.py` passed (both the prior thinking-card mobile polish check and new live activity chip check).

Work in progress / still to do
- Visual QA in an actual browser on iPhone-sized viewports (screenshots or visual pass) — not performed yet.
- Minor polish tasks remaining (optional):
  - add subtle per-kind icon glyph (wrench/terminal/check/agent) in chip UI (kept intentionally minimal).
  - tune exit TTL after testing in real sessions (900ms is a chosen heuristic).
  - finalize command truncation thresholds if required by design.
- Commit / push status:
  - Earlier orb/copy pack changes were committed and pushed.
  - The live-activity changes are implemented in working tree (files modified); if you want these persisted to main, they should be committed and pushed. (If you want me to perform that commit+push, I can do so on request.)

Acceptance criteria status (mapped to user's list)
- Generic concept chips replaced during active work: Done (live activities used).
- Chip labels reflect actual tool calls/commands: Done (commandLabel formatting pulls `command`/`test_command` inputs; tool name normalization kept truthful).
- Chips appear on tool/command start & disappear on stop: Done (reactive store-driven, with brief exit window).
- Realtime updates during agent execution: Done (driven reactively from `data.store.part`).
- Chip count capped by breakpoint and overflow handled: Done (desktop 5, tablet 4, mobile 3; `+N active` overflow).
- Long command truncation: Done (truthful truncation with ellipsis for display only).
- Premium animation preserved: Done (enter/exit hooks and mapped status styles preserved).
- `tools/doctor.py` passes: Done (new check added and passes).

## Relevant files / directories

High-priority files that were read, created or modified (these are the only places to touch for this task):

- Event source / store (read, not modified):
  - packages/app/src/context/global-sdk.tsx (SSE event source)
  - packages/app/src/context/global-sync/event-reducer.ts (reduces `message.part.updated` into `store.part`)
  - packages/app/src/context/global-sync.tsx / global-sync bootstrap files (where store becomes available)

- Where live activities are derived and passed to the theater (modified):
  - packages/ui/src/components/session-turn.tsx
    - derives `acts` (LiveActivity list) from `data.store.part` for current assistant messages
    - maps `ToolPart` → LiveActivity
    - implements just-finished TTL window and de-dup/prioritization
    - passes `activities={acts()}` into ThinkingTheater

- Where chips are rendered & styled (modified):
  - packages/ui/src/components/thinking-theater.tsx
    - `LiveActivity` type definition
    - `activities?: LiveActivity[]` prop
    - cap logic, shown slice, overflow `+N active` chip
    - fallback behavior when `activities` is empty
  - packages/ui/src/components/thinking-theater.css
    - chip row: non-wrapping, stable height, `data-kind`/`data-status` selectors
    - visual states: running/completed/error mappings and mono font for commands

- Copy pack and title/subtext (previous task; read/kept intact for titles):
  - packages/ui/src/lib/thinking-wording.ts (phase-specific title/subtext; used for title/subtext fallback; unchanged for chips)

- Utilities & types (read):
  - packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/types.gen.ts (ToolPart, ToolState types used to map fields)
  - packages/ui/src/context/data.tsx (useData store type shape)
  - packages/ui/src/components/message-part.tsx, basic-tool.tsx (existing tool UI mapping functions consulted)

- Documentation & doctor (created):
  - .local/implementation-live-activity-chips.md
  - tools/doctor_checks/live_activity_chips.py
  - tools/doctor.py (updated to include the new check)

- Other (left unchanged / intentionally not touched):
  - Orb and earlier thinking layout files (already completed in earlier tasks)
  - Any unrelated files outside those listed above

---

If you hand this off to another agent, these are the most important starting points:

- Look at `session-turn.tsx` to see how `acts()` is derived from `data.store.part`. That is the single point of truth builder for LiveActivity.
- Look at `thinking-theater.tsx` to see how `activities` are rendered, capped, and styled; this is where to tweak UI or animation.
- If you need to confirm event fidelity, inspect `global-sync/event-reducer.ts` (app side) to confirm `message.part.updated` arrives as expected and to verify `part.state` fields are present (start/end/inputs).
- For visual QA, load a real session where tool parts are firing (commands, verify, read) and inspect the chip lifecycle and overflow on small viewports.
- If committing & pushing is desired, stage these modified files and push to main; ensure CI (`bun turbo typecheck` + `python3 tools/doctor.py`) is green.

---

warnings: Runtime artifact files under .opencode and __pycache__ remain uncommitted by design.
